classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License

---
substitutions:
  pypi: |-

# OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions

{{ pypi }}

This library contains generated code for the semantic conventions defined by the OpenTelemetry specification.

## Installation

```
pip install opentelemetry-semantic-conventions
```

## Code Generation

These files were generated automatically from code in [semconv].
To regenerate the code, run `../scripts/semconv/generate.sh`.

To build against a new release or specific commit of [opentelemetry-specification],
update the `SPEC_VERSION` variable in
`../scripts/semconv/generate.sh`. Then run the script and commit the changes.

## References

- [OpenTelemetry Project](https://opentelemetry.io/)
- [OpenTelemetry Semantic Conventions Definitions](https://github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conventions/blob/main/docs/README.md)
- [generate.sh script](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-python/blob/main/scripts/semconv/generate.sh)

[opentelemetry-specification]: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification
[semconv]: https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-python/tree/main/scripts/semconv

documentation:
Measures the duration of outbound RPC
Instrument: histogram
Unit: ms
Note: While streaming RPCs may record this metric as start-of-batch
    to end-of-batch, it's hard to interpret in practice.

    **Streaming**: N/A.

documentation:
Measures the size of RPC request messages (uncompressed)
Instrument: histogram
Unit: By
Note: **Streaming**: Recorded per message in a streaming batch.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.rpc_metrics.RPC_CLIENT_REQUESTS_PER_RPC
documentation:
Measures the number of messages received per RPC
Instrument: histogram
Unit: {count}
Note: Should be 1 for all non-streaming RPCs.

    **Streaming**: This metric is required for server and client streaming RPCs.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.k8s_attributes.K8S_CLUSTER_UID
documentation:
A pseudo-ID for the cluster, set to the UID of the `kube-system` namespace.
Note: K8s doesn't have support for obtaining a cluster ID. If this is ever
    added, we will recommend collecting the `k8s.cluster.uid` through the
    official APIs. In the meantime, we are able to use the `uid` of the
    `kube-system` namespace as a proxy for cluster ID. Read on for the
    rationale.

    Every object created in a K8s cluster is assigned a distinct UID. The
    `kube-system` namespace is used by Kubernetes itself and will exist
    for the lifetime of the cluster. Using the `uid` of the `kube-system`
    namespace is a reasonable proxy for the K8s ClusterID as it will only
    change if the cluster is rebuilt. Furthermore, Kubernetes UIDs are
    UUIDs as standardized by
    [ISO/IEC 9834-8 and ITU-T X.667](https://www.itu.int/ITU-T/studygroups/com17/oid.html).
    Which states:

    > If generated according to one of the mechanisms defined in Rec.
      ITU-T X.667 | ISO/IEC 9834-8, a UUID is either guaranteed to be
      different from all other UUIDs generated before 3603 A.D., or is
      extremely likely to be different (depending on the mechanism chosen).

    Therefore, UIDs between clusters should be extremely unlikely to
    conflict.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CIPHER
documentation:
String indicating the [cipher](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5246#appendix-A.5) used during the current connection.
Note: The values allowed for `tls.cipher` MUST be one of the `Descriptions` of the [registered TLS Cipher Suits](https://www.iana.org/assignments/tls-parameters/tls-parameters.xhtml#table-tls-parameters-4).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE
documentation: PEM-encoded stand-alone certificate offered by the client. This is usually mutually-exclusive of `client.certificate_chain` since this value also exists in that list.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_CERTIFICATE_CHAIN
documentation: Array of PEM-encoded certificates that make up the certificate chain offered by the client. This is usually mutually-exclusive of `client.certificate` since that value should be the first certificate in the chain.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_HASH_MD5
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the MD5 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the client. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_HASH_SHA1
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the SHA1 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the client. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_HASH_SHA256
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the SHA256 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the client. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_ISSUER
documentation: Distinguished name of [subject](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5280#section-4.1.2.6) of the issuer of the x.509 certificate presented by the client.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_JA3
documentation: A hash that identifies clients based on how they perform an SSL/TLS handshake.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_NOT_AFTER
documentation: Date/Time indicating when client certificate is no longer considered valid.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_NOT_BEFORE
documentation: Date/Time indicating when client certificate is first considered valid.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_SERVER_NAME
documentation: Also called an SNI, this tells the server which hostname to which the client is attempting to connect to.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_SUBJECT
documentation: Distinguished name of subject of the x.509 certificate presented by the client.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CLIENT_SUPPORTED_CIPHERS
documentation: Array of ciphers offered by the client during the client hello.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_CURVE
documentation: String indicating the curve used for the given cipher, when applicable.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_ESTABLISHED
documentation: Boolean flag indicating if the TLS negotiation was successful and transitioned to an encrypted tunnel.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_NEXT_PROTOCOL
documentation: String indicating the protocol being tunneled. Per the values in the [IANA registry](https://www.iana.org/assignments/tls-extensiontype-values/tls-extensiontype-values.xhtml#alpn-protocol-ids), this string should be lower case.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_PROTOCOL_NAME
documentation: Normalized lowercase protocol name parsed from original string of the negotiated [SSL/TLS protocol version](https://www.openssl.org/docs/man1.1.1/man3/SSL_get_version.html#RETURN-VALUES).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_PROTOCOL_VERSION
documentation: Numeric part of the version parsed from the original string of the negotiated [SSL/TLS protocol version](https://www.openssl.org/docs/man1.1.1/man3/SSL_get_version.html#RETURN-VALUES).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_RESUMED
documentation: Boolean flag indicating if this TLS connection was resumed from an existing TLS negotiation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_CERTIFICATE
documentation: PEM-encoded stand-alone certificate offered by the server. This is usually mutually-exclusive of `server.certificate_chain` since this value also exists in that list.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_CERTIFICATE_CHAIN
documentation: Array of PEM-encoded certificates that make up the certificate chain offered by the server. This is usually mutually-exclusive of `server.certificate` since that value should be the first certificate in the chain.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_HASH_MD5
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the MD5 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the server. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_HASH_SHA1
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the SHA1 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the server. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_HASH_SHA256
documentation: Certificate fingerprint using the SHA256 digest of DER-encoded version of certificate offered by the server. For consistency with other hash values, this value should be formatted as an uppercase hash.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_ISSUER
documentation: Distinguished name of [subject](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5280#section-4.1.2.6) of the issuer of the x.509 certificate presented by the client.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_JA3S
documentation: A hash that identifies servers based on how they perform an SSL/TLS handshake.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_NOT_AFTER
documentation: Date/Time indicating when server certificate is no longer considered valid.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_NOT_BEFORE
documentation: Date/Time indicating when server certificate is first considered valid.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TLS_SERVER_SUBJECT
documentation: Distinguished name of subject of the x.509 certificate presented by the server.

documentation: ssl.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.tls_attributes.TlsProtocolNameValues.TLS
documentation: tls.

documentation: The name of the service provider that performs the flag evaluation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.feature_flag_attributes.FEATURE_FLAG_VARIANT
documentation:
SHOULD be a semantic identifier for a value. If one is unavailable, a stringified version of the value can be used.
Note: A semantic identifier, commonly referred to as a variant, provides a means
    for referring to a value without including the value itself. This can
    provide additional context for understanding the meaning behind a value.
    For example, the variant `red` maybe be used for the value `#c05543`.

    A stringified version of the value can be used in situations where a
    semantic identifier is unavailable. String representation of the value
    should be determined by the implementer.

opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_CPU_LOGICAL_COUNT
opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_CPU_PHYSICAL_COUNT

documentation:
Seconds each logical CPU spent on each mode
Instrument: counter
Unit: s

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_CPU_UTILIZATION
documentation:
Difference in system.cpu.time since the last measurement, divided by the elapsed time and number of logical CPUs
Instrument: gauge
Unit: 1

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_DISK_IO
documentation:
Instrument: counter
Unit: By

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_DISK_IO_TIME
documentation:
Time disk spent activated
Instrument: counter
Unit: s
Note: The real elapsed time ("wall clock") used in the I/O path (time from operations running in parallel are not counted). Measured as:

    - Linux: Field 13 from [procfs-diskstats](https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/ABI/testing/procfs-diskstats)
    - Windows: The complement of
      ["Disk\% Idle Time"](https://learn.microsoft.com/archive/blogs/askcore/windows-performance-monitor-disk-counters-explained#windows-performance-monitor-disk-counters-explained)
      performance counter: `uptime * (100 - "Disk\% Idle Time") / 100`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_DISK_MERGED
documentation:
Instrument: counter
Unit: {operation}

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_DISK_OPERATION_TIME
documentation:
Sum of the time each operation took to complete
Instrument: counter
Unit: s
Note: Because it is the sum of time each request took, parallel-issued requests each contribute to make the count grow. Measured as:

    - Linux: Fields 7 & 11 from [procfs-diskstats](https://www.kernel.org/doc/Documentation/ABI/testing/procfs-diskstats)
    - Windows: "Avg. Disk sec/Read" perf counter multiplied by "Disk Reads/sec" perf counter (similar for Writes).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_DISK_OPERATIONS
documentation:
Instrument: counter
Unit: {operation}

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_FILESYSTEM_USAGE
documentation:
Instrument: updowncounter
Unit: By

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_FILESYSTEM_UTILIZATION
documentation:
Instrument: gauge
Unit: 1

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_LINUX_MEMORY_AVAILABLE
documentation:
An estimate of how much memory is available for starting new applications, without causing swapping
Instrument: updowncounter
Unit: By
Note: This is an alternative to `system.memory.usage` metric with `state=free`.
    Linux starting from 3.14 exports "available" memory. It takes "free" memory as a baseline, and then factors in kernel-specific values.
    This is supposed to be more accurate than just "free" memory.
    For reference, see the calculations [here](https://superuser.com/a/980821).
    See also `MemAvailable` in [/proc/meminfo](https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man5/proc.5.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_MEMORY_LIMIT
documentation:
Total memory available in the system
Instrument: updowncounter
Unit: By
Note: Its value SHOULD equal the sum of `system.memory.state` over all states.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_MEMORY_USAGE
documentation:
Reports memory in use by state
Instrument: updowncounter
Unit: By
Note: The sum over all `system.memory.state` values SHOULD equal the total memory
    available on the system, that is `system.memory.limit`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_MEMORY_UTILIZATION
documentation:
Instrument: gauge
Unit: 1

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_NETWORK_CONNECTIONS
documentation:
Instrument: updowncounter
Unit: {connection}

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_NETWORK_DROPPED
documentation:
Count of packets that are dropped or discarded even though there was no error
Instrument: counter
Unit: {packet}
Note: Measured as:

    - Linux: the `drop` column in `/proc/dev/net` ([source](https://web.archive.org/web/20180321091318/http://www.onlamp.com/pub/a/linux/2000/11/16/LinuxAdmin.html))
    - Windows: [`InDiscards`/`OutDiscards`](https://docs.microsoft.com/windows/win32/api/netioapi/ns-netioapi-mib_if_row2)
      from [`GetIfEntry2`](https://docs.microsoft.com/windows/win32/api/netioapi/nf-netioapi-getifentry2).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_NETWORK_ERRORS
documentation:
Count of network errors detected
Instrument: counter
Unit: {error}
Note: Measured as:

    - Linux: the `errs` column in `/proc/dev/net` ([source](https://web.archive.org/web/20180321091318/http://www.onlamp.com/pub/a/linux/2000/11/16/LinuxAdmin.html)).
    - Windows: [`InErrors`/`OutErrors`](https://docs.microsoft.com/windows/win32/api/netioapi/ns-netioapi-mib_if_row2)
      from [`GetIfEntry2`](https://docs.microsoft.com/windows/win32/api/netioapi/nf-netioapi-getifentry2).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_NETWORK_IO
documentation:
Instrument: counter
Unit: By

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_NETWORK_PACKETS
documentation:
Instrument: counter
Unit: {packet}

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_PAGING_FAULTS

Instrument: counter
Unit: {fault}

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.system_metrics.SYSTEM_PAGING_OPERATIONS

Instrument: counter

Instrument: updowncounter
Unit: By

documentation:

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.http_metrics.HTTP_CLIENT_OPEN_CONNECTIONS
opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.metrics.http_metrics.HTTP_CLIENT_REQUEST_BODY_SIZE
documentation:
Size of HTTP client request bodies
Instrument: histogram
Unit: By
Note: The size of the request payload body in bytes. This is the number of bytes transferred excluding headers and is often, but not always, present as the [Content-Length](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#field.content-length) header. For requests using transport encoding, this should be the compressed size.

Instrument: histogram
Unit: By

This is the number of bytes transferred excluding headers and is often, but not always, present as the [Content-Length](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#field.content-length) header. For requests using transport encoding, this should be the compressed size.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_CONSISTENCY_LEVEL
documentation: The consistency level of the query. Based on consistency values from [CQL](https://docs.datastax.com/en/cassandra-oss/3.0/cassandra/dml/dmlConfigConsistency.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_COORDINATOR_DC
documentation: The data center of the coordinating node for a query.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_COORDINATOR_ID
documentation: The ID of the coordinating node for a query.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_IDEMPOTENCE
documentation: Whether or not the query is idempotent.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_PAGE_SIZE
documentation: The fetch size used for paging, i.e. how many rows will be returned at once.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_SPECULATIVE_EXECUTION_COUNT
documentation: The number of times a query was speculatively executed. Not set or `0` if the query was not executed speculatively.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CASSANDRA_TABLE
documentation:
The name of the primary Cassandra table that the operation is acting upon, including the keyspace name (if applicable).
Note: This mirrors the db.sql.table attribute but references cassandra rather than sql. It is not recommended to attempt any client-side parsing of `db.statement` just to get this property, but it should be set if it is provided by the library being instrumented. If the operation is acting upon an anonymous table, or more than one table, this value MUST NOT be set.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_CONNECTION_STRING
documentation: Deprecated: "Replaced by `server.address` and `server.port`.".

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_CLIENT_ID
documentation: Unique Cosmos client instance id.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_CONNECTION_MODE
documentation: Cosmos client connection mode.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_CONTAINER
documentation: Cosmos DB container name.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_OPERATION_TYPE
documentation: CosmosDB Operation Type.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_REQUEST_CHARGE
documentation: RU consumed for that operation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_REQUEST_CONTENT_LENGTH
documentation: Request payload size in bytes.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_STATUS_CODE
documentation: Cosmos DB status code.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_COSMOSDB_SUB_STATUS_CODE
documentation: Cosmos DB sub status code.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_ELASTICSEARCH_CLUSTER_NAME
documentation: Represents the identifier of an Elasticsearch cluster.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_ELASTICSEARCH_NODE_NAME
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `db.instance.id`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_ELASTICSEARCH_PATH_PARTS_TEMPLATE
documentation:
A dynamic value in the url path.
Note: Many Elasticsearch url paths allow dynamic values. These SHOULD be recorded in span attributes in the format `db.elasticsearch.path_parts.<key>`, where `<key>` is the url path part name. The implementation SHOULD reference the [elasticsearch schema](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/elastic/elasticsearch-specification/main/output/schema/schema.json) in order to map the path part values to their names.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_INSTANCE_ID
documentation: An identifier (address, unique name, or any other identifier) of the database instance that is executing queries or mutations on the current connection. This is useful in cases where the database is running in a clustered environment and the instrumentation is able to record the node executing the query. The client may obtain this value in databases like MySQL using queries like `select @@hostname`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_JDBC_DRIVER_CLASSNAME
documentation: Deprecated: Removed as not used.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_MONGODB_COLLECTION
documentation: The MongoDB collection being accessed within the database stated in `db.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_MSSQL_INSTANCE_NAME
documentation:
The Microsoft SQL Server [instance name](https://docs.microsoft.com/sql/connect/jdbc/building-the-connection-url?view=sql-server-ver15) connecting to. This name is used to determine the port of a named instance.
Note: If setting a `db.mssql.instance_name`, `server.port` is no longer required (but still recommended if non-standard).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_NAME
documentation:
This attribute is used to report the name of the database being accessed. For commands that switch the database, this should be set to the target database (even if the command fails).
Note: In some SQL databases, the database name to be used is called "schema name". In case there are multiple layers that could be considered for database name (e.g. Oracle instance name and schema name), the database name to be used is the more specific layer (e.g. Oracle schema name).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_OPERATION
documentation:
The name of the operation being executed, e.g. the [MongoDB command name](https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/#database-operations) such as `findAndModify`, or the SQL keyword.
Note: When setting this to an SQL keyword, it is not recommended to attempt any client-side parsing of `db.statement` just to get this property, but it should be set if the operation name is provided by the library being instrumented. If the SQL statement has an ambiguous operation, or performs more than one operation, this value may be omitted.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_REDIS_DATABASE_INDEX
documentation: The index of the database being accessed as used in the [`SELECT` command](https://redis.io/commands/select), provided as an integer. To be used instead of the generic `db.name` attribute.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_SQL_TABLE
documentation:
The name of the primary table that the operation is acting upon, including the database name (if applicable).
Note: It is not recommended to attempt any client-side parsing of `db.statement` just to get this property, but it should be set if it is provided by the library being instrumented. If the operation is acting upon an anonymous table, or more than one table, this value MUST NOT be set.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_STATEMENT
documentation: The database statement being executed.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_SYSTEM
documentation: An identifier for the database management system (DBMS) product being used. See below for a list of well-known identifiers.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DB_USER
documentation: Username for accessing the database.

documentation: serial.

documentation: local_serial.

documentation: Gateway (HTTP) connections mode.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.db_attributes.DbCosmosdbConnectionModeValues.DIRECT
documentation: Direct connection.

documentation: invalid.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.file_attributes.FILE_DIRECTORY
documentation: Directory where the file is located. It should include the drive letter, when appropriate.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.file_attributes.FILE_EXTENSION
documentation:
File extension, excluding the leading dot.
Note: When the file name has multiple extensions (example.tar.gz), only the last one should be captured ("gz", not "tar.gz").

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.file_attributes.FILE_NAME
documentation: Name of the file including the extension, without the directory.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.file_attributes.FILE_PATH
documentation: Full path to the file, including the file name. It should include the drive letter, when appropriate.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.file_attributes.FILE_SIZE
documentation: File size in bytes.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_COLDSTART
documentation: A boolean that is true if the serverless function is executed for the first time (aka cold-start).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_CRON
documentation: A string containing the schedule period as [Cron Expression](https://docs.oracle.com/cd/E12058_01/doc/doc.1014/e12030/cron_expressions.htm).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_COLLECTION
documentation: The name of the source on which the triggering operation was performed. For example, in Cloud Storage or S3 corresponds to the bucket name, and in Cosmos DB to the database name.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_NAME
documentation: The document name/table subjected to the operation. For example, in Cloud Storage or S3 is the name of the file, and in Cosmos DB the table name.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_OPERATION
documentation: Describes the type of the operation that was performed on the data.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_DOCUMENT_TIME
documentation: A string containing the time when the data was accessed in the [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format expressed in [UTC](https://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_INSTANCE
documentation:
The execution environment ID as a string, that will be potentially reused for other invocations to the same function/function version.
Note: * **AWS Lambda:** Use the (full) log stream name.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_INVOCATION_ID
documentation: The invocation ID of the current function invocation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_INVOKED_NAME
documentation:
The name of the invoked function.
Note: SHOULD be equal to the `faas.name` resource attribute of the invoked function.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_INVOKED_PROVIDER
documentation:
The cloud provider of the invoked function.
Note: SHOULD be equal to the `cloud.provider` resource attribute of the invoked function.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_INVOKED_REGION
documentation:
The cloud region of the invoked function.
Note: SHOULD be equal to the `cloud.region` resource attribute of the invoked function.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_MAX_MEMORY
documentation:
The amount of memory available to the serverless function converted to Bytes.
Note: It's recommended to set this attribute since e.g. too little memory can easily stop a Java AWS Lambda function from working correctly. On AWS Lambda, the environment variable `AWS_LAMBDA_FUNCTION_MEMORY_SIZE` provides this information (which must be multiplied by 1,048,576).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_NAME
documentation:
The name of the single function that this runtime instance executes.
Note: This is the name of the function as configured/deployed on the FaaS
    platform and is usually different from the name of the callback
    function (which may be stored in the
    [`code.namespace`/`code.function`](/docs/general/attributes.md#source-code-attributes)
    span attributes).

    For some cloud providers, the above definition is ambiguous. The following
    definition of function name MUST be used for this attribute
    (and consequently the span name) for the listed cloud providers/products:

    * **Azure:**  The full name `<FUNCAPP>/<FUNC>`, i.e., function app name
      followed by a forward slash followed by the function name (this form
      can also be seen in the resource JSON for the function).
      This means that a span attribute MUST be used, as an Azure function
      app can host multiple functions that would usually share
      a TracerProvider (see also the `cloud.resource_id` attribute).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_TIME
documentation: A string containing the function invocation time in the [ISO 8601](https://www.iso.org/iso-8601-date-and-time-format.html) format expressed in [UTC](https://www.w3.org/TR/NOTE-datetime).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_TRIGGER
documentation: Type of the trigger which caused this function invocation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FAAS_VERSION
documentation:
The immutable version of the function being executed.
Note: Depending on the cloud provider and platform, use:

    * **AWS Lambda:** The [function version](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-versions.html)
      (an integer represented as a decimal string).
    * **Google Cloud Run (Services):** The [revision](https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/managing/revisions)
      (i.e., the function name plus the revision suffix).
    * **Google Cloud Functions:** The value of the
      [`K_REVISION` environment variable](https://cloud.google.com/functions/docs/env-var#runtime_environment_variables_set_automatically).
    * **Azure Functions:** Not applicable. Do not set this attribute.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasDocumentOperationValues.INSERT
documentation: When a new object is created.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasDocumentOperationValues.EDIT
documentation: When an object is modified.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasDocumentOperationValues.DELETE
documentation: When an object is deleted.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasInvokedProviderValues.ALIBABA_CLOUD
documentation: Alibaba Cloud.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasInvokedProviderValues.AWS
documentation: Amazon Web Services.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasInvokedProviderValues.AZURE
documentation: Microsoft Azure.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasInvokedProviderValues.GCP
documentation: Google Cloud Platform.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasInvokedProviderValues.TENCENT_CLOUD
documentation: Tencent Cloud.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasTriggerValues.DATASOURCE
documentation: A response to some data source operation such as a database or filesystem read/write.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasTriggerValues.HTTP
documentation: To provide an answer to an inbound HTTP request.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasTriggerValues.PUBSUB
documentation: A function is set to be executed when messages are sent to a messaging system.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasTriggerValues.TIMER
documentation: A function is scheduled to be executed regularly.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.faas_attributes.FaasTriggerValues.OTHER
documentation: If none of the others apply.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.event_attributes.EVENT_NAME
documentation:
Identifies the class / type of event.
Note: Event names are subject to the same rules as [attribute names](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-specification/tree/v1.31.0/specification/common/attribute-naming.md). Notably, event names are namespaced to avoid collisions and provide a clean separation of semantics for events in separate domains like browser, mobile, and kubernetes.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.code_attributes.CODE_COLUMN
documentation: The column number in `code.filepath` best representing the operation. It SHOULD point within the code unit named in `code.function`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.code_attributes.CODE_FILEPATH
documentation: The source code file name that identifies the code unit as uniquely as possible (preferably an absolute file path).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.code_attributes.CODE_FUNCTION
documentation: The method or function name, or equivalent (usually rightmost part of the code unit's name).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.code_attributes.CODE_LINENO
documentation: The line number in `code.filepath` best representing the operation. It SHOULD point within the code unit named in `code.function`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.code_attributes.CODE_NAMESPACE
documentation: The "namespace" within which `code.function` is defined. Usually the qualified class or module name, such that `code.namespace` + some separator + `code.function` form a unique identifier for the code unit.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.code_attributes.CODE_STACKTRACE
documentation: A stacktrace as a string in the natural representation for the language runtime. The representation is to be determined and documented by each language SIG.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_CONNECT_RPC_ERROR_CODE
documentation: The [error codes](https://connect.build/docs/protocol/#error-codes) of the Connect request. Error codes are always string values.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_CONNECT_RPC_REQUEST_METADATA_TEMPLATE
documentation:
Connect request metadata, `<key>` being the normalized Connect Metadata key (lowercase), the value being the metadata values.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which metadata values are to be captured. Including all request metadata values can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_CONNECT_RPC_RESPONSE_METADATA_TEMPLATE
documentation:
Connect response metadata, `<key>` being the normalized Connect Metadata key (lowercase), the value being the metadata values.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which metadata values are to be captured. Including all response metadata values can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_GRPC_REQUEST_METADATA_TEMPLATE
documentation:
gRPC request metadata, `<key>` being the normalized gRPC Metadata key (lowercase), the value being the metadata values.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which metadata values are to be captured. Including all request metadata values can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_GRPC_RESPONSE_METADATA_TEMPLATE
documentation:
gRPC response metadata, `<key>` being the normalized gRPC Metadata key (lowercase), the value being the metadata values.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which metadata values are to be captured. Including all response metadata values can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_GRPC_STATUS_CODE
documentation: The [numeric status code](https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/v1.33.2/doc/statuscodes.md) of the gRPC request.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_JSONRPC_ERROR_CODE
documentation: `error.code` property of response if it is an error response.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_JSONRPC_ERROR_MESSAGE
documentation: `error.message` property of response if it is an error response.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_JSONRPC_REQUEST_ID
documentation: `id` property of request or response. Since protocol allows id to be int, string, `null` or missing (for notifications), value is expected to be cast to string for simplicity. Use empty string in case of `null` value. Omit entirely if this is a notification.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_JSONRPC_VERSION
documentation: Protocol version as in `jsonrpc` property of request/response. Since JSON-RPC 1.0 doesn't specify this, the value can be omitted.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_METHOD
documentation:
The name of the (logical) method being called, must be equal to the $method part in the span name.
Note: This is the logical name of the method from the RPC interface perspective, which can be different from the name of any implementing method/function. The `code.function` attribute may be used to store the latter (e.g., method actually executing the call on the server side, RPC client stub method on the client side).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_SERVICE
documentation:
The full (logical) name of the service being called, including its package name, if applicable.
Note: This is the logical name of the service from the RPC interface perspective, which can be different from the name of any implementing class. The `code.namespace` attribute may be used to store the latter (despite the attribute name, it may include a class name; e.g., class with method actually executing the call on the server side, RPC client stub class on the client side).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.rpc_attributes.RPC_SYSTEM

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.gcp_attributes.GCP_CLOUD_RUN_JOB_EXECUTION
documentation: The name of the Cloud Run [execution](https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/managing/job-executions) being run for the Job, as set by the [`CLOUD_RUN_EXECUTION`](https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/container-contract#jobs-env-vars) environment variable.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.gcp_attributes.GCP_CLOUD_RUN_JOB_TASK_INDEX
documentation: The index for a task within an execution as provided by the [`CLOUD_RUN_TASK_INDEX`](https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/container-contract#jobs-env-vars) environment variable.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.gcp_attributes.GCP_GCE_INSTANCE_HOSTNAME
documentation: The hostname of a GCE instance. This is the full value of the default or [custom hostname](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/instances/custom-hostname-vm).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.gcp_attributes.GCP_GCE_INSTANCE_NAME
documentation: The instance name of a GCE instance. This is the value provided by `host.name`, the visible name of the instance in the Cloud Console UI, and the prefix for the default hostname of the instance as defined by the [default internal DNS name](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/internal-dns#instance-fully-qualified-domain-names).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.log_attributes.LOG_FILE_NAME
documentation: The basename of the file.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.log_attributes.LOG_FILE_NAME_RESOLVED
documentation: The basename of the file, with symlinks resolved.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.log_attributes.LOG_FILE_PATH
documentation: The full path to the file.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.log_attributes.LOG_FILE_PATH_RESOLVED
documentation: The full path to the file, with symlinks resolved.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.log_attributes.LOG_IOSTREAM
documentation: The stream associated with the log. See below for a list of well-known values.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.log_attributes.LOG_RECORD_UID
documentation:
A unique identifier for the Log Record.
Note: If an id is provided, other log records with the same id will be considered duplicates and can be removed safely. This means, that two distinguishable log records MUST have different values.
    The id MAY be an [Universally Unique Lexicographically Sortable Identifier (ULID)](https://github.com/ulid/spec), but other identifiers (e.g. UUID) may be used as needed.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.log_attributes.LogIostreamValues.STDOUT
documentation: Logs from stdout stream.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.log_attributes.LogIostreamValues.STDERR
documentation: Events from stderr stream.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.device_attributes.DEVICE_ID
documentation:
A unique identifier representing the device.
Note: The device identifier MUST only be defined using the values outlined below. This value is not an advertising identifier and MUST NOT be used as such. On iOS (Swift or Objective-C), this value MUST be equal to the [vendor identifier](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uidevice/1620059-identifierforvendor). On Android (Java or Kotlin), this value MUST be equal to the Firebase Installation ID or a globally unique UUID which is persisted across sessions in your application. More information can be found [here](https://developer.android.com/training/articles/user-data-ids) on best practices and exact implementation details. Caution should be taken when storing personal data or anything which can identify a user. GDPR and data protection laws may apply, ensure you do your own due diligence.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.device_attributes.DEVICE_MANUFACTURER
documentation:
The name of the device manufacturer.
Note: The Android OS provides this field via [Build](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/os/Build#MANUFACTURER). iOS apps SHOULD hardcode the value `Apple`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.device_attributes.DEVICE_MODEL_IDENTIFIER
documentation:
The model identifier for the device.
Note: It's recommended this value represents a machine-readable version of the model identifier rather than the market or consumer-friendly name of the device.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.device_attributes.DEVICE_MODEL_NAME
documentation:
The marketing name for the device model.
Note: It's recommended this value represents a human-readable version of the device model rather than a machine-readable alternative.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_COMMAND
documentation: The command used to launch the process (i.e. the command name). On Linux based systems, can be set to the zeroth string in `proc/[pid]/cmdline`. On Windows, can be set to the first parameter extracted from `GetCommandLineW`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_COMMAND_ARGS
documentation: All the command arguments (including the command/executable itself) as received by the process. On Linux-based systems (and some other Unixoid systems supporting procfs), can be set according to the list of null-delimited strings extracted from `proc/[pid]/cmdline`. For libc-based executables, this would be the full argv vector passed to `main`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_COMMAND_LINE
documentation: The full command used to launch the process as a single string representing the full command. On Windows, can be set to the result of `GetCommandLineW`. Do not set this if you have to assemble it just for monitoring; use `process.command_args` instead.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_CONTEXT_SWITCH_TYPE
documentation: Specifies whether the context switches for this data point were voluntary or involuntary.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_CPU_STATE
documentation: The CPU state for this data point. A process SHOULD be characterized _either_ by data points with no `state` labels, _or only_ data points with `state` labels.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_EXECUTABLE_NAME
documentation: The name of the process executable. On Linux based systems, can be set to the `Name` in `proc/[pid]/status`. On Windows, can be set to the base name of `GetProcessImageFileNameW`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_EXECUTABLE_PATH
documentation: The full path to the process executable. On Linux based systems, can be set to the target of `proc/[pid]/exe`. On Windows, can be set to the result of `GetProcessImageFileNameW`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_OWNER
documentation: The username of the user that owns the process.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_PAGING_FAULT_TYPE
documentation: The type of page fault for this data point. Type `major` is for major/hard page faults, and `minor` is for minor/soft page faults.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_PARENT_PID
documentation: Parent Process identifier (PPID).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_PID
documentation: Process identifier (PID).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_RUNTIME_DESCRIPTION
documentation: An additional description about the runtime of the process, for example a specific vendor customization of the runtime environment.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_RUNTIME_NAME
documentation: The name of the runtime of this process. For compiled native binaries, this SHOULD be the name of the compiler.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.PROCESS_RUNTIME_VERSION
documentation: The version of the runtime of this process, as returned by the runtime without modification.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessContextSwitchTypeValues.VOLUNTARY
documentation: voluntary.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessContextSwitchTypeValues.INVOLUNTARY
documentation: involuntary.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessCpuStateValues.SYSTEM
documentation: system.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessCpuStateValues.USER
documentation: user.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessCpuStateValues.WAIT
documentation: wait.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessPagingFaultTypeValues.MAJOR
documentation: major.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.process_attributes.ProcessPagingFaultTypeValues.MINOR
documentation: minor.

documentation: The [`service.name`](/docs/resource/README.md#service) of the remote service. SHOULD be equal to the actual `service.name` resource attribute of the remote service if any.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_BATCH_MESSAGE_COUNT
documentation:
The number of messages sent, received, or processed in the scope of the batching operation.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD NOT set `messaging.batch.message_count` on spans that operate with a single message. When a messaging client library supports both batch and single-message API for the same operation, instrumentations SHOULD use `messaging.batch.message_count` for batching APIs and SHOULD NOT use it for single-message APIs.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_CLIENT_ID
documentation: A unique identifier for the client that consumes or produces a message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_ANONYMOUS
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message destination is anonymous (could be unnamed or have auto-generated name).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_NAME
documentation:
The message destination name.
Note: Destination name SHOULD uniquely identify a specific queue, topic or other entity within the broker. If
    the broker doesn't have such notion, the destination name SHOULD uniquely identify the broker.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_PARTITION_ID
documentation: The identifier of the partition messages are sent to or received from, unique within the `messaging.destination.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_TEMPLATE
documentation:
Low cardinality representation of the messaging destination name.
Note: Destination names could be constructed from templates. An example would be a destination name involving a user name or product id. Although the destination name in this case is of high cardinality, the underlying template is of low cardinality and can be effectively used for grouping and aggregation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_TEMPORARY
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message destination is temporary and might not exist anymore after messages are processed.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_PUBLISH_ANONYMOUS
documentation: A boolean that is true if the publish message destination is anonymous (could be unnamed or have auto-generated name).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_DESTINATION_PUBLISH_NAME
documentation:
The name of the original destination the message was published to.
Note: The name SHOULD uniquely identify a specific queue, topic, or other entity within the broker. If
    the broker doesn't have such notion, the original destination name SHOULD uniquely identify the broker.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_EVENTHUBS_CONSUMER_GROUP
documentation: The name of the consumer group the event consumer is associated with.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_EVENTHUBS_MESSAGE_ENQUEUED_TIME
documentation: The UTC epoch seconds at which the message has been accepted and stored in the entity.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_GCP_PUBSUB_MESSAGE_ORDERING_KEY
documentation: The ordering key for a given message. If the attribute is not present, the message does not have an ordering key.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_CONSUMER_GROUP
documentation: Name of the Kafka Consumer Group that is handling the message. Only applies to consumers, not producers.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_DESTINATION_PARTITION
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `messaging.destination.partition.id`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_MESSAGE_KEY
documentation:
Message keys in Kafka are used for grouping alike messages to ensure they're processed on the same partition. They differ from `messaging.message.id` in that they're not unique. If the key is `null`, the attribute MUST NOT be set.
Note: If the key type is not string, it's string representation has to be supplied for the attribute. If the key has no unambiguous, canonical string form, don't include its value.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_MESSAGE_OFFSET
documentation: The offset of a record in the corresponding Kafka partition.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_KAFKA_MESSAGE_TOMBSTONE
documentation: A boolean that is true if the message is a tombstone.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_BODY_SIZE
documentation:
The size of the message body in bytes.
Note: This can refer to both the compressed or uncompressed body size. If both sizes are known, the uncompressed
    body size should be used.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_CONVERSATION_ID
documentation: The conversation ID identifying the conversation to which the message belongs, represented as a string. Sometimes called "Correlation ID".

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_ENVELOPE_SIZE
documentation:
The size of the message body and metadata in bytes.
Note: This can refer to both the compressed or uncompressed size. If both sizes are known, the uncompressed
    size should be used.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_MESSAGE_ID
documentation: A value used by the messaging system as an identifier for the message, represented as a string.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_OPERATION
documentation:
A string identifying the kind of messaging operation.
Note: If a custom value is used, it MUST be of low cardinality.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_RABBITMQ_DESTINATION_ROUTING_KEY
documentation: RabbitMQ message routing key.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_RABBITMQ_MESSAGE_DELIVERY_TAG
documentation: RabbitMQ message delivery tag.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_CLIENT_GROUP
documentation: Name of the RocketMQ producer/consumer group that is handling the message. The client type is identified by the SpanKind.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_CONSUMPTION_MODEL
documentation: Model of message consumption. This only applies to consumer spans.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_DELAY_TIME_LEVEL
documentation: The delay time level for delay message, which determines the message delay time.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_DELIVERY_TIMESTAMP
documentation: The timestamp in milliseconds that the delay message is expected to be delivered to consumer.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_GROUP
documentation: It is essential for FIFO message. Messages that belong to the same message group are always processed one by one within the same consumer group.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_KEYS
documentation: Key(s) of message, another way to mark message besides message id.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_TAG
documentation: The secondary classifier of message besides topic.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_MESSAGE_TYPE
documentation: Type of message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_ROCKETMQ_NAMESPACE
documentation: Namespace of RocketMQ resources, resources in different namespaces are individual.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_SERVICEBUS_DESTINATION_SUBSCRIPTION_NAME
documentation: The name of the subscription in the topic messages are received from.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_SERVICEBUS_DISPOSITION_STATUS
documentation: Describes the [settlement type](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/service-bus-messaging/message-transfers-locks-settlement#peeklock).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_SERVICEBUS_MESSAGE_DELIVERY_COUNT
documentation: Number of deliveries that have been attempted for this message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_SERVICEBUS_MESSAGE_ENQUEUED_TIME
documentation: The UTC epoch seconds at which the message has been accepted and stored in the entity.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MESSAGING_SYSTEM
documentation: An identifier for the messaging system being used. See below for a list of well-known identifiers.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingOperationValues.PUBLISH
documentation: One or more messages are provided for publishing to an intermediary. If a single message is published, the context of the "Publish" span can be used as the creation context and no "Create" span needs to be created.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.messaging_attributes.MessagingOperationValues.CREATE

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_INSTANCE_ID
documentation:
The string ID of the service instance.
Note: MUST be unique for each instance of the same `service.namespace,service.name` pair (in other words
    `service.namespace,service.name,service.instance.id` triplet MUST be globally unique). The ID helps to
    distinguish instances of the same service that exist at the same time (e.g. instances of a horizontally scaled
    service).

    Implementations, such as SDKs, are recommended to generate a random Version 1 or Version 4 [RFC
    4122](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc4122.txt) UUID, but are free to use an inherent unique ID as the source of
    this value if stability is desirable. In that case, the ID SHOULD be used as source of a UUID Version 5 and
    SHOULD use the following UUID as the namespace: `4d63009a-8d0f-11ee-aad7-4c796ed8e320`.

    UUIDs are typically recommended, as only an opaque value for the purposes of identifying a service instance is
    needed. Similar to what can be seen in the man page for the
    [`/etc/machine-id`](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/machine-id.html) file, the underlying
    data, such as pod name and namespace should be treated as confidential, being the user's choice to expose it
    or not via another resource attribute.

    For applications running behind an application server (like unicorn), we do not recommend using one identifier
    for all processes participating in the application. Instead, it's recommended each division (e.g. a worker
    thread in unicorn) to have its own instance.id.

    It's not recommended for a Collector to set `service.instance.id` if it can't unambiguously determine the
    service instance that is generating that telemetry. For instance, creating an UUID based on `pod.name` will
    likely be wrong, as the Collector might not know from which container within that pod the telemetry originated.
    However, Collectors can set the `service.instance.id` if they can unambiguously determine the service instance
    for that telemetry. This is typically the case for scraping receivers, as they know the target address and
    port.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_NAME
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_NAME`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_NAMESPACE
documentation:
A namespace for `service.name`.
Note: A string value having a meaning that helps to distinguish a group of services, for example the team name that owns a group of services. `service.name` is expected to be unique within the same namespace. If `service.namespace` is not specified in the Resource then `service.name` is expected to be unique for all services that have no explicit namespace defined (so the empty/unspecified namespace is simply one more valid namespace). Zero-length namespace string is assumed equal to unspecified namespace.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_VERSION
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_VERSION`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.dns_attributes.DNS_QUESTION_NAME
documentation:
The name being queried.
Note: If the name field contains non-printable characters (below 32 or above 126), those characters should be represented as escaped base 10 integers (\DDD). Back slashes and quotes should be escaped. Tabs, carriage returns, and line feeds should be converted to \t, \r, and \n respectively.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloud_attributes.CLOUD_ACCOUNT_ID
documentation: The cloud account ID the resource is assigned to.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloud_attributes.CLOUD_AVAILABILITY_ZONE
documentation:
Cloud regions often have multiple, isolated locations known as zones to increase availability. Availability zone represents the zone where the resource is running.
Note: Availability zones are called "zones" on Alibaba Cloud and Google Cloud.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloud_attributes.CLOUD_PLATFORM
documentation:
The cloud platform in use.
Note: The prefix of the service SHOULD match the one specified in `cloud.provider`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloud_attributes.CLOUD_PROVIDER
documentation: Name of the cloud provider.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloud_attributes.CLOUD_REGION
documentation:
The geographical region the resource is running.
Note: Refer to your provider's docs to see the available regions, for example [Alibaba Cloud regions](https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/40654.htm), [AWS regions](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/global-infrastructure/regions_az/), [Azure regions](https://azure.microsoft.com/global-infrastructure/geographies/), [Google Cloud regions](https://cloud.google.com/about/locations), or [Tencent Cloud regions](https://www.tencentcloud.com/document/product/213/6091).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloud_attributes.CLOUD_RESOURCE_ID
documentation:
Cloud provider-specific native identifier of the monitored cloud resource (e.g. an [ARN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html) on AWS, a [fully qualified resource ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/rest/api/resources/resources/get-by-id) on Azure, a [full resource name](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/resource_names#full_resource_name) on GCP).
Note: On some cloud providers, it may not be possible to determine the full ID at startup,
    so it may be necessary to set `cloud.resource_id` as a span attribute instead.

    The exact value to use for `cloud.resource_id` depends on the cloud provider.
    The following well-known definitions MUST be used if you set this attribute and they apply:

    * **AWS Lambda:** The function [ARN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html).
      Take care not to use the "invoked ARN" directly but replace any
      [alias suffix](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-aliases.html)
      with the resolved function version, as the same runtime instance may be invokable with
      multiple different aliases.
    * **GCP:** The [URI of the resource](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/full-resource-names)
    * **Azure:** The [Fully Qualified Resource ID](https://docs.microsoft.com/rest/api/resources/resources/get-by-id) of the invoked function,
      *not* the function app, having the form
      `/subscriptions/<SUBSCIPTION_GUID>/resourceGroups/<RG>/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/<FUNCAPP>/functions/<FUNC>`.
      This means that a span attribute MUST be used, as an Azure function app can host multiple functions that would usually share
      a TracerProvider.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.browser_attributes.BROWSER_BRANDS
documentation:
Array of brand name and version separated by a space.
Note: This value is intended to be taken from the [UA client hints API](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#interface) (`navigator.userAgentData.brands`).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.browser_attributes.BROWSER_LANGUAGE
documentation:
Preferred language of the user using the browser.
Note: This value is intended to be taken from the Navigator API `navigator.language`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.browser_attributes.BROWSER_MOBILE
documentation:
A boolean that is true if the browser is running on a mobile device.
Note: This value is intended to be taken from the [UA client hints API](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#interface) (`navigator.userAgentData.mobile`). If unavailable, this attribute SHOULD be left unset.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.browser_attributes.BROWSER_PLATFORM
documentation:
The platform on which the browser is running.
Note: This value is intended to be taken from the [UA client hints API](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#interface) (`navigator.userAgentData.platform`). If unavailable, the legacy `navigator.platform` API SHOULD NOT be used instead and this attribute SHOULD be left unset in order for the values to be consistent.
    The list of possible values is defined in the [W3C User-Agent Client Hints specification](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#sec-ch-ua-platform). Note that some (but not all) of these values can overlap with values in the [`os.type` and `os.name` attributes](./os.md). However, for consistency, the values in the `browser.platform` attribute should capture the exact value that the user agent provides.

Note: When observed from the destination side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `source.address` SHOULD represent the source address behind any intermediaries, for example proxies, if it's available.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_ARCH
documentation: The CPU architecture the host system is running on.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_CPU_CACHE_L2_SIZE
documentation: The amount of level 2 memory cache available to the processor (in Bytes).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_CPU_FAMILY
documentation: Family or generation of the CPU.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_CPU_MODEL_ID
documentation: Model identifier. It provides more granular information about the CPU, distinguishing it from other CPUs within the same family.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_CPU_MODEL_NAME
documentation: Model designation of the processor.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_CPU_STEPPING
documentation: Stepping or core revisions.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_CPU_VENDOR_ID
documentation:
Processor manufacturer identifier. A maximum 12-character string.
Note: [CPUID](https://wiki.osdev.org/CPUID) command returns the vendor ID string in EBX, EDX and ECX registers. Writing these to memory in this order results in a 12-character string.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_ID
documentation: Unique host ID. For Cloud, this must be the instance_id assigned by the cloud provider. For non-containerized systems, this should be the `machine-id`. See the table below for the sources to use to determine the `machine-id` based on operating system.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_IMAGE_ID
documentation: VM image ID or host OS image ID. For Cloud, this value is from the provider.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_IMAGE_NAME
documentation: Name of the VM image or OS install the host was instantiated from.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_IMAGE_VERSION
documentation: The version string of the VM image or host OS as defined in [Version Attributes](/docs/resource/README.md#version-attributes).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_IP
documentation:
Available IP addresses of the host, excluding loopback interfaces.
Note: IPv4 Addresses MUST be specified in dotted-quad notation. IPv6 addresses MUST be specified in the [RFC 5952](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5952.html) format.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_MAC
documentation:
Available MAC addresses of the host, excluding loopback interfaces.
Note: MAC Addresses MUST be represented in [IEEE RA hexadecimal form](https://standards.ieee.org/wp-content/uploads/import/documents/tutorials/eui.pdf): as hyphen-separated octets in uppercase hexadecimal form from most to least significant.

documentation: Name of the host. On Unix systems, it may contain what the hostname command returns, or the fully qualified hostname, or another name specified by the user.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HOST_TYPE
documentation: Type of host. For Cloud, this must be the machine type.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HostArchValues.PPC32
documentation: 32-bit PowerPC.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HostArchValues.PPC64
documentation: 64-bit PowerPC.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HostArchValues.S390X

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.host_attributes.HostArchValues.X86

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.enduser_attributes.ENDUSER_ID
documentation: Username or client_id extracted from the access token or [Authorization](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7235#section-4.2) header in the inbound request from outside the system.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.enduser_attributes.ENDUSER_ROLE
documentation: Actual/assumed role the client is making the request under extracted from token or application security context.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.enduser_attributes.ENDUSER_SCOPE
documentation: Scopes or granted authorities the client currently possesses extracted from token or application security context. The value would come from the scope associated with an [OAuth 2.0 Access Token](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-3.3) or an attribute value in a [SAML 2.0 Assertion](http://docs.oasis-open.org/security/saml/Post2.0/sstc-saml-tech-overview-2.0.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.user_agent_attributes.USER_AGENT_NAME
documentation:
Name of the user-agent extracted from original. Usually refers to the browser's name.
Note: [Example](https://www.whatsmyua.info) of extracting browser's name from original string. In the case of using a user-agent for non-browser products, such as microservices with multiple names/versions inside the `user_agent.original`, the most significant name SHOULD be selected. In such a scenario it should align with `user_agent.version`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.user_agent_attributes.USER_AGENT_ORIGINAL
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.user_agent_attributes.USER_AGENT_ORIGINAL`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.user_agent_attributes.USER_AGENT_VERSION
documentation:
Version of the user-agent extracted from original. Usually refers to the browser's version.
Note: [Example](https://www.whatsmyua.info) of extracting browser's version from original string. In the case of using a user-agent for non-browser products, such as microservices with multiple names/versions inside the `user_agent.original`, the most significant version SHOULD be selected. In such a scenario it should align with `user_agent.name`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloudevents_attributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_ID
documentation: The [event_id](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#id) uniquely identifies the event.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloudevents_attributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_SOURCE
documentation: The [source](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#source-1) identifies the context in which an event happened.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloudevents_attributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_SPEC_VERSION

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloudevents_attributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_SUBJECT
documentation: The [subject](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#subject) of the event in the context of the event producer (identified by source).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.cloudevents_attributes.CLOUDEVENTS_EVENT_TYPE
documentation: The [event_type](https://github.com/cloudevents/spec/blob/v1.0.2/cloudevents/spec.md#type) contains a value describing the type of event related to the originating occurrence.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.deployment_attributes.DEPLOYMENT_ENVIRONMENT
documentation:
Name of the [deployment environment](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/Deployment_environment) (aka deployment tier).
Note: `deployment.environment` does not affect the uniqueness constraints defined through
    the `service.namespace`, `service.name` and `service.instance.id` resource attributes.
    This implies that resources carrying the following attribute combinations MUST be
    considered to be identifying the same service:

    * `service.name=frontend`, `deployment.environment=production`
    * `service.name=frontend`, `deployment.environment=staging`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.destination_attributes.DESTINATION_ADDRESS
documentation:
Destination address - domain name if available without reverse DNS lookup; otherwise, IP address or Unix domain socket name.
Note: When observed from the source side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `destination.address` SHOULD represent the destination address behind any intermediaries, for example proxies, if it's available.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.destination_attributes.DESTINATION_PORT
documentation: Destination port number.
