WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_DOMAIN
documentation:
Domain extracted from the `url.full`, such as "opentelemetry.io".
Note: In some cases a URL may refer to an IP and/or port directly, without a domain name. In this case, the IP address would go to the domain field. If the URL contains a [literal IPv6 address](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2732#section-2) enclosed by `[` and `]`, the `[` and `]` characters should also be captured in the domain field.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_EXTENSION
documentation:
The file extension extracted from the `url.full`, excluding the leading dot.
Note: The file extension is only set if it exists, as not every url has a file extension. When the file name has multiple extensions `example.tar.gz`, only the last one should be captured `gz`, not `tar.gz`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_FRAGMENT
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.url_attributes.URL_FRAGMENT`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_FULL
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.url_attributes.URL_FULL`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_ORIGINAL
documentation:
Unmodified original URL as seen in the event source.
Note: In network monitoring, the observed URL may be a full URL, whereas in access logs, the URL is often just represented as a path. This field is meant to represent the URL as it was observed, complete or not.
    `url.original` might contain credentials passed via URL in form of `https://username:password@www.example.com/`. In such case password and username SHOULD NOT be redacted and attribute's value SHOULD remain the same.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_PATH
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.url_attributes.URL_PATH`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_PORT
documentation: Port extracted from the `url.full`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_QUERY
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.url_attributes.URL_QUERY`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_REGISTERED_DOMAIN
documentation:
The highest registered url domain, stripped of the subdomain.
Note: This value can be determined precisely with the [public suffix list](http://publicsuffix.org). For example, the registered domain for `foo.example.com` is `example.com`. Trying to approximate this by simply taking the last two labels will not work well for TLDs such as `co.uk`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_SCHEME
documentation: Deprecated in favor of stable {py:const}`opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.url_attributes.URL_SCHEME`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_SUBDOMAIN
documentation:
The subdomain portion of a fully qualified domain name includes all of the names except the host name under the registered_domain. In a partially qualified domain, or if the qualification level of the full name cannot be determined, subdomain contains all of the names below the registered domain.
Note: The subdomain portion of `www.east.mydomain.co.uk` is `east`. If the domain has multiple levels of subdomain, such as `sub2.sub1.example.com`, the subdomain field should contain `sub2.sub1`, with no trailing period.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.url_attributes.URL_TOP_LEVEL_DOMAIN
documentation:
The effective top level domain (eTLD), also known as the domain suffix, is the last part of the domain name. For example, the top level domain for example.com is `com`.
Note: This value can be determined precisely with the [public suffix list](http://publicsuffix.org).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_COMMAND
documentation:
The command used to run the container (i.e. the command name).
Note: If using embedded credentials or sensitive data, it is recommended to remove them to prevent potential leakage.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_COMMAND_ARGS
documentation: All the command arguments (including the command/executable itself) run by the container. [2].

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_COMMAND_LINE
documentation: The full command run by the container as a single string representing the full command. [2].

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_CPU_STATE
documentation: The CPU state for this data point.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_ID
documentation: Container ID. Usually a UUID, as for example used to [identify Docker containers](https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/run/#container-identification). The UUID might be abbreviated.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_IMAGE_ID
documentation:
Runtime specific image identifier. Usually a hash algorithm followed by a UUID.
Note: Docker defines a sha256 of the image id; `container.image.id` corresponds to the `Image` field from the Docker container inspect [API](https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.43/#tag/Container/operation/ContainerInspect) endpoint.
    K8s defines a link to the container registry repository with digest `"imageID": "registry.azurecr.io /namespace/service/dockerfile@sha256:bdeabd40c3a8a492eaf9e8e44d0ebbb84bac7ee25ac0cf8a7159d25f62555625"`.
    The ID is assinged by the container runtime and can vary in different environments. Consider using `oci.manifest.digest` if it is important to identify the same image in different environments/runtimes.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_IMAGE_NAME
documentation: Name of the image the container was built on.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_IMAGE_REPO_DIGESTS
documentation:
Repo digests of the container image as provided by the container runtime.
Note: [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.43/#tag/Image/operation/ImageInspect) and [CRI](https://github.com/kubernetes/cri-api/blob/c75ef5b473bbe2d0a4fc92f82235efd665ea8e9f/pkg/apis/runtime/v1/api.proto#L1237-L1238) report those under the `RepoDigests` field.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_IMAGE_TAGS
documentation: Container image tags. An example can be found in [Docker Image Inspect](https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.43/#tag/Image/operation/ImageInspect). Should be only the `<tag>` section of the full name for example from `registry.example.com/my-org/my-image:<tag>`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_LABEL_TEMPLATE
documentation: Container labels, `<key>` being the label name, the value being the label value.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_LABELS_TEMPLATE
documentation: Deprecated: Replaced by `container.label`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_NAME
documentation: Container name used by container runtime.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.CONTAINER_RUNTIME
documentation: The container runtime managing this container.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.ContainerCpuStateValues.USER
documentation: When tasks of the cgroup are in user mode (Linux). When all container processes are in user mode (Windows).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.ContainerCpuStateValues.SYSTEM
documentation: When CPU is used by the system (host OS).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.container_attributes.ContainerCpuStateValues.KERNEL
documentation: When tasks of the cgroup are in kernel mode (Linux). When all container processes are in kernel mode (Windows).

Note: Follows [OCI Image Manifest Specification](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/manifest.md), and specifically the [Digest property](https://github.com/opencontainers/image-spec/blob/main/descriptor.md#digests).
    An example can be found in [Example Image Manifest](https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/manifest-v2-2/#example-image-manifest).

Note: Official auto instrumentation agents and distributions SHOULD set the `telemetry.distro.name` attribute to

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.client_attributes.CLIENT_ADDRESS
documentation:
Client address - domain name if available without reverse DNS lookup; otherwise, IP address or Unix domain socket name.
Note: When observed from the server side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `client.address` SHOULD represent the client address behind any intermediaries,  for example proxies, if it's available.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.client_attributes.CLIENT_PORT
documentation:
Client port number.
Note: When observed from the server side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `client.port` SHOULD represent the client port behind any intermediaries,  for example proxies, if it's available.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_ECS_CLUSTER_ARN
documentation: The ARN of an [ECS cluster](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/clusters.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_ECS_CONTAINER_ARN
documentation: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an [ECS container instance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/ECS_instances.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_ECS_LAUNCHTYPE
documentation: The [launch type](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/launch_types.html) for an ECS task.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_ECS_TASK_ARN
documentation: The ARN of a running [ECS task](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/ecs-account-settings.html#ecs-resource-ids).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_ECS_TASK_FAMILY
documentation: The family name of the [ECS task definition](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/task_definitions.html) used to create the ECS task.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_ECS_TASK_ID
documentation: The ID of a running ECS task. The ID MUST be extracted from `task.arn`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_ECS_TASK_REVISION
documentation: The revision for the task definition used to create the ECS task.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_EKS_CLUSTER_ARN
documentation: The ARN of an EKS cluster.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_LAMBDA_INVOKED_ARN
documentation:
The full invoked ARN as provided on the `Context` passed to the function (`Lambda-Runtime-Invoked-Function-Arn` header on the `/runtime/invocation/next` applicable).
Note: This may be different from `cloud.resource_id` if an alias is involved.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_LOG_GROUP_ARNS
documentation:
The Amazon Resource Name(s) (ARN) of the AWS log group(s).
Note: See the [log group ARN format documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/iam-access-control-overview-cwl.html#CWL_ARN_Format).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_LOG_GROUP_NAMES
documentation:
The name(s) of the AWS log group(s) an application is writing to.
Note: Multiple log groups must be supported for cases like multi-container applications, where a single application has sidecar containers, and each write to their own log group.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_LOG_STREAM_ARNS
documentation:
The ARN(s) of the AWS log stream(s).
Note: See the [log stream ARN format documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/iam-access-control-overview-cwl.html#CWL_ARN_Format). One log group can contain several log streams, so these ARNs necessarily identify both a log group and a log stream.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_LOG_STREAM_NAMES
documentation: The name(s) of the AWS log stream(s) an application is writing to.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_REQUEST_ID
documentation: The AWS request ID as returned in the response headers `x-amz-request-id` or `x-amz-requestid`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_S3_BUCKET
documentation:
The S3 bucket name the request refers to. Corresponds to the `--bucket` parameter of the [S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/index.html) operations.
Note: The `bucket` attribute is applicable to all S3 operations that reference a bucket, i.e. that require the bucket name as a mandatory parameter.
    This applies to almost all S3 operations except `list-buckets`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_S3_COPY_SOURCE
documentation:
The source object (in the form `bucket`/`key`) for the copy operation.
Note: The `copy_source` attribute applies to S3 copy operations and corresponds to the `--copy-source` parameter
    of the [copy-object operation within the S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/copy-object.html).
    This applies in particular to the following operations:

    - [copy-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/copy-object.html)
    - [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_S3_DELETE
documentation:
The delete request container that specifies the objects to be deleted.
Note: The `delete` attribute is only applicable to the [delete-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/delete-object.html) operation.
    The `delete` attribute corresponds to the `--delete` parameter of the
    [delete-objects operation within the S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/delete-objects.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_S3_KEY
documentation:
The S3 object key the request refers to. Corresponds to the `--key` parameter of the [S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/index.html) operations.
Note: The `key` attribute is applicable to all object-related S3 operations, i.e. that require the object key as a mandatory parameter.
    This applies in particular to the following operations:

    - [copy-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/copy-object.html)
    - [delete-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/delete-object.html)
    - [get-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/get-object.html)
    - [head-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/head-object.html)
    - [put-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/put-object.html)
    - [restore-object](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/restore-object.html)
    - [select-object-content](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/select-object-content.html)
    - [abort-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/abort-multipart-upload.html)
    - [complete-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/complete-multipart-upload.html)
    - [create-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/create-multipart-upload.html)
    - [list-parts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/list-parts.html)
    - [upload-part](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html)
    - [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_S3_PART_NUMBER
documentation:
The part number of the part being uploaded in a multipart-upload operation. This is a positive integer between 1 and 10,000.
Note: The `part_number` attribute is only applicable to the [upload-part](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html)
    and [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html) operations.
    The `part_number` attribute corresponds to the `--part-number` parameter of the
    [upload-part operation within the S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv._incubating.attributes.aws_attributes.AWS_S3_UPLOAD_ID
documentation:
Upload ID that identifies the multipart upload.
Note: The `upload_id` attribute applies to S3 multipart-upload operations and corresponds to the `--upload-id` parameter
    of the [S3 API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/index.html) multipart operations.
    This applies in particular to the following operations:

    - [abort-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/abort-multipart-upload.html)
    - [complete-multipart-upload](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/complete-multipart-upload.html)
    - [list-parts](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/list-parts.html)
    - [upload-part](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part.html)
    - [upload-part-copy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/s3api/upload-part-copy.html).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_NAME
documentation:
Logical name of the service.
Note: MUST be the same for all instances of horizontally scaled services. If the value was not specified, SDKs MUST fallback to `unknown_service:` concatenated with [`process.executable.name`](process.md#process), e.g. `unknown_service:bash`. If `process.executable.name` is not available, the value MUST be set to `unknown_service`.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.service_attributes.SERVICE_VERSION
documentation: The version string of the service API or implementation. The format is not defined by these conventions.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.exception_attributes.EXCEPTION_ESCAPED
documentation:
SHOULD be set to true if the exception event is recorded at a point where it is known that the exception is escaping the scope of the span.
Note: An exception is considered to have escaped (or left) the scope of a span,
    if that span is ended while the exception is still logically "in flight".
    This may be actually "in flight" in some languages (e.g. if the exception
    is passed to a Context manager's `__exit__` method in Python) but will
    usually be caught at the point of recording the exception in most languages.

    It is usually not possible to determine at the point where an exception is thrown
    whether it will escape the scope of a span.
    However, it is trivial to know that an exception
    will escape, if one checks for an active exception just before ending the span,
    as done in the [example for recording span exceptions](#recording-an-exception).

    It follows that an exception may still escape the scope of the span
    even if the `exception.escaped` attribute was not set or set to false,
    since the event might have been recorded at a time where it was not
    clear whether the exception will escape.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.exception_attributes.EXCEPTION_MESSAGE
documentation: The exception message.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.exception_attributes.EXCEPTION_STACKTRACE
documentation: A stacktrace as a string in the natural representation for the language runtime. The representation is to be determined and documented by each language SIG.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.exception_attributes.EXCEPTION_TYPE
documentation: The type of the exception (its fully-qualified class name, if applicable). The dynamic type of the exception should be preferred over the static type in languages that support it.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.error_attributes.ERROR_TYPE
documentation:
Describes a class of error the operation ended with.
Note: The `error.type` SHOULD be predictable and SHOULD have low cardinality.
    Instrumentations SHOULD document the list of errors they report.

    The cardinality of `error.type` within one instrumentation library SHOULD be low.
    Telemetry consumers that aggregate data from multiple instrumentation libraries and applications
    should be prepared for `error.type` to have high cardinality at query time when no
    additional filters are applied.

    If the operation has completed successfully, instrumentations SHOULD NOT set `error.type`.

    If a specific domain defines its own set of error identifiers (such as HTTP or gRPC status codes),
    it's RECOMMENDED to:

    * Use a domain-specific attribute
    * Set `error.type` to capture all errors, regardless of whether they are defined within the domain-specific set or not.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.error_attributes.ErrorTypeValues.OTHER
documentation: A fallback error value to be used when the instrumentation doesn't define a custom value.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.network_attributes.NETWORK_LOCAL_ADDRESS
documentation: Local address of the network connection - IP address or Unix domain socket name.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.network_attributes.NETWORK_LOCAL_PORT
documentation: Local port number of the network connection.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.network_attributes.NETWORK_PEER_ADDRESS
documentation: Peer address of the network connection - IP address or Unix domain socket name.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.network_attributes.NETWORK_PEER_PORT
documentation: Peer port number of the network connection.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.network_attributes.NETWORK_PROTOCOL_NAME
documentation:
[OSI application layer](https://osi-model.com/application-layer/) or non-OSI equivalent.
Note: The value SHOULD be normalized to lowercase.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.network_attributes.NETWORK_PROTOCOL_VERSION
documentation:
The actual version of the protocol used for network communication.
Note: If protocol version is subject to negotiation (for example using [ALPN](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc7301.html)), this attribute SHOULD be set to the negotiated version. If the actual protocol version is not known, this attribute SHOULD NOT be set.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.network_attributes.NETWORK_TRANSPORT
documentation:
[OSI transport layer](https://osi-model.com/transport-layer/) or [inter-process communication method](https://wikipedia.org/wiki/Inter-process_communication).
Note: The value SHOULD be normalized to lowercase.

    Consider always setting the transport when setting a port number, since
    a port number is ambiguous without knowing the transport. For example
    different processes could be listening on TCP port 12345 and UDP port 12345.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.network_attributes.NETWORK_TYPE
documentation:
[OSI network layer](https://osi-model.com/network-layer/) or non-OSI equivalent.
Note: The value SHOULD be normalized to lowercase.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.network_attributes.NetworkTransportValues.TCP
documentation: TCP.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.url_attributes.URL_FRAGMENT
documentation: The [URI fragment](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986#section-3.5) component.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.url_attributes.URL_FULL
documentation:
Absolute URL describing a network resource according to [RFC3986](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986).
Note: For network calls, URL usually has `scheme://host[:port][path][?query][#fragment]` format, where the fragment is not transmitted over HTTP, but if it is known, it SHOULD be included nevertheless.
    `url.full` MUST NOT contain credentials passed via URL in form of `https://username:password@www.example.com/`. In such case username and password SHOULD be redacted and attribute's value SHOULD be `https://REDACTED:REDACTED@www.example.com/`.
    `url.full` SHOULD capture the absolute URL when it is available (or can be reconstructed). Sensitive content provided in `url.full` SHOULD be scrubbed when instrumentations can identify it.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.url_attributes.URL_PATH
documentation:
The [URI path](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986#section-3.3) component.
Note: Sensitive content provided in `url.path` SHOULD be scrubbed when instrumentations can identify it.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.url_attributes.URL_QUERY
documentation:
The [URI query](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986#section-3.4) component.
Note: Sensitive content provided in `url.query` SHOULD be scrubbed when instrumentations can identify it.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.url_attributes.URL_SCHEME
documentation: The [URI scheme](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3986#section-3.1) component identifying the used protocol.

documentation:
Server domain name if available without reverse DNS lookup; otherwise, IP address or Unix domain socket name.
Note: When observed from the client side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `server.address` SHOULD represent the server address behind any intermediaries, for example proxies, if it's available.

Note: When observed from the client side, and when communicating through an intermediary, `server.port` SHOULD represent the server port behind any intermediaries, for example proxies, if it's available.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.http_attributes.HTTP_REQUEST_HEADER_TEMPLATE
documentation:
HTTP request headers, `<key>` being the normalized HTTP Header name (lowercase), the value being the header values.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which headers are to be captured. Including all request headers can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.
    The `User-Agent` header is already captured in the `user_agent.original` attribute. Users MAY explicitly configure instrumentations to capture them even though it is not recommended.
    The attribute value MUST consist of either multiple header values as an array of strings or a single-item array containing a possibly comma-concatenated string, depending on the way the HTTP library provides access to headers.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.http_attributes.HTTP_REQUEST_METHOD
documentation:
HTTP request method.
Note: HTTP request method value SHOULD be "known" to the instrumentation.
    By default, this convention defines "known" methods as the ones listed in [RFC9110](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9110.html#name-methods)
    and the PATCH method defined in [RFC5789](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5789.html).

    If the HTTP request method is not known to instrumentation, it MUST set the `http.request.method` attribute to `_OTHER`.

    If the HTTP instrumentation could end up converting valid HTTP request methods to `_OTHER`, then it MUST provide a way to override
    the list of known HTTP methods. If this override is done via environment variable, then the environment variable MUST be named
    OTEL_INSTRUMENTATION_HTTP_KNOWN_METHODS and support a comma-separated list of case-sensitive known HTTP methods
    (this list MUST be a full override of the default known method, it is not a list of known methods in addition to the defaults).

    HTTP method names are case-sensitive and `http.request.method` attribute value MUST match a known HTTP method name exactly.
    Instrumentations for specific web frameworks that consider HTTP methods to be case insensitive, SHOULD populate a canonical equivalent.
    Tracing instrumentations that do so, MUST also set `http.request.method_original` to the original value.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.http_attributes.HTTP_REQUEST_METHOD_ORIGINAL
documentation: Original HTTP method sent by the client in the request line.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.http_attributes.HTTP_REQUEST_RESEND_COUNT
documentation:
The ordinal number of request resending attempt (for any reason, including redirects).
Note: The resend count SHOULD be updated each time an HTTP request gets resent by the client, regardless of what was the cause of the resending (e.g. redirection, authorization failure, 503 Server Unavailable, network issues, or any other).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.http_attributes.HTTP_RESPONSE_HEADER_TEMPLATE
documentation:
HTTP response headers, `<key>` being the normalized HTTP Header name (lowercase), the value being the header values.
Note: Instrumentations SHOULD require an explicit configuration of which headers are to be captured. Including all response headers can be a security risk - explicit configuration helps avoid leaking sensitive information.
    Users MAY explicitly configure instrumentations to capture them even though it is not recommended.
    The attribute value MUST consist of either multiple header values as an array of strings or a single-item array containing a possibly comma-concatenated string, depending on the way the HTTP library provides access to headers.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.http_attributes.HTTP_RESPONSE_STATUS_CODE
documentation: [HTTP response status code](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7231#section-6).

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.http_attributes.HTTP_ROUTE
documentation:
The matched route, that is, the path template in the format used by the respective server framework.
Note: MUST NOT be populated when this is not supported by the HTTP server framework as the route attribute should have low-cardinality and the URI path can NOT substitute it.
    SHOULD include the [application root](/docs/http/http-spans.md#http-server-definitions) if there is one.

WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TELEMETRY_SDK_LANGUAGE
documentation: The language of the telemetry SDK.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TELEMETRY_SDK_NAME
documentation:
The name of the telemetry SDK as defined above.
Note: The OpenTelemetry SDK MUST set the `telemetry.sdk.name` attribute to `opentelemetry`.
    If another SDK, like a fork or a vendor-provided implementation, is used, this SDK MUST set the
    `telemetry.sdk.name` attribute to the fully-qualified class or module name of this SDK's main entry point
    or another suitable identifier depending on the language.
    The identifier `opentelemetry` is reserved and MUST NOT be used in this case.
    All custom identifiers SHOULD be stable across different versions of an implementation.

variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TELEMETRY_SDK_VERSION
documentation: The version string of the telemetry SDK.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.CPP
documentation: cpp.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.DOTNET
documentation: dotnet.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.ERLANG
documentation: erlang.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.GO
documentation: go.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.JAVA
documentation: java.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.NODEJS
documentation: nodejs.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.PHP
documentation: php.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.PYTHON
documentation: python.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.RUBY
documentation: ruby.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.RUST
documentation: rust.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.SWIFT
documentation: swift.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.attributes.telemetry_attributes.TelemetrySdkLanguageValues.WEBJS
documentation: webjs.

pylint: disable=too-many-lines

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.SCHEMA_URL
documentation: The URL of the OpenTelemetry schema for these keys and values.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.BROWSER_BRANDS
documentation:
Array of brand name and version separated by a space.
Note: This value is intended to be taken from the [UA client hints API](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#interface) (`navigator.userAgentData.brands`).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.BROWSER_PLATFORM
documentation:
The platform on which the browser is running.
Note: This value is intended to be taken from the [UA client hints API](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#interface) (`navigator.userAgentData.platform`). If unavailable, the legacy `navigator.platform` API SHOULD NOT be used instead and this attribute SHOULD be left unset in order for the values to be consistent.
The list of possible values is defined in the [W3C User-Agent Client Hints specification](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#sec-ch-ua-platform). Note that some (but not all) of these values can overlap with values in the [`os.type` and `os.name` attributes](./os.md). However, for consistency, the values in the `browser.platform` attribute should capture the exact value that the user agent provides.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.BROWSER_MOBILE
documentation:
A boolean that is true if the browser is running on a mobile device.
Note: This value is intended to be taken from the [UA client hints API](https://wicg.github.io/ua-client-hints/#interface) (`navigator.userAgentData.mobile`). If unavailable, this attribute SHOULD be left unset.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.BROWSER_LANGUAGE
documentation:
Preferred language of the user using the browser.
Note: This value is intended to be taken from the Navigator API `navigator.language`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.USER_AGENT_ORIGINAL
documentation:
Full user-agent string provided by the browser.
Note: The user-agent value SHOULD be provided only from browsers that do not have a mechanism to retrieve brands and platform individually from the User-Agent Client Hints API. To retrieve the value, the legacy `navigator.userAgent` API can be used.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CLOUD_PROVIDER
documentation: Name of the cloud provider.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CLOUD_ACCOUNT_ID
documentation: The cloud account ID the resource is assigned to.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CLOUD_REGION
documentation:
The geographical region the resource is running.
Note: Refer to your provider's docs to see the available regions, for example [Alibaba Cloud regions](https://www.alibabacloud.com/help/doc-detail/40654.htm), [AWS regions](https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/global-infrastructure/regions_az/), [Azure regions](https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/global-infrastructure/geographies/), [Google Cloud regions](https://cloud.google.com/about/locations), or [Tencent Cloud regions](https://www.tencentcloud.com/document/product/213/6091).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CLOUD_RESOURCE_ID
documentation:
Cloud provider-specific native identifier of the monitored cloud resource (e.g. an [ARN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html) on AWS, a [fully qualified resource ID](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/resources/resources/get-by-id) on Azure, a [full resource name](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/resource_names#full_resource_name) on GCP).
Note: On some cloud providers, it may not be possible to determine the full ID at startup,
so it may be necessary to set `cloud.resource_id` as a span attribute instead.

The exact value to use for `cloud.resource_id` depends on the cloud provider.
The following well-known definitions MUST be used if you set this attribute and they apply:

* **AWS Lambda:** The function [ARN](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/general/latest/gr/aws-arns-and-namespaces.html).
  Take care not to use the "invoked ARN" directly but replace any
  [alias suffix](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-aliases.html)
  with the resolved function version, as the same runtime instance may be invokable with
  multiple different aliases.
* **GCP:** The [URI of the resource](https://cloud.google.com/iam/docs/full-resource-names)
* **Azure:** The [Fully Qualified Resource ID](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/resources/resources/get-by-id) of the invoked function,
  *not* the function app, having the form
  `/subscriptions/<SUBSCIPTION_GUID>/resourceGroups/<RG>/providers/Microsoft.Web/sites/<FUNCAPP>/functions/<FUNC>`.
  This means that a span attribute MUST be used, as an Azure function app can host multiple functions that would usually share
  a TracerProvider.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CLOUD_AVAILABILITY_ZONE
documentation:
Cloud regions often have multiple, isolated locations known as zones to increase availability. Availability zone represents the zone where the resource is running.
Note: Availability zones are called "zones" on Alibaba Cloud and Google Cloud.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CLOUD_PLATFORM
documentation:
The cloud platform in use.
Note: The prefix of the service SHOULD match the one specified in `cloud.provider`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_ECS_CONTAINER_ARN
documentation: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an [ECS container instance](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/ECS_instances.html).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_ECS_CLUSTER_ARN
documentation: The ARN of an [ECS cluster](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/clusters.html).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_ECS_LAUNCHTYPE
documentation: The [launch type](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/launch_types.html) for an ECS task.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_ECS_TASK_ARN
documentation: The ARN of an [ECS task definition](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/task_definitions.html).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_ECS_TASK_FAMILY
documentation: The task definition family this task definition is a member of.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_ECS_TASK_REVISION
documentation: The revision for this task definition.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_EKS_CLUSTER_ARN
documentation: The ARN of an EKS cluster.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_LOG_GROUP_NAMES
documentation:
The name(s) of the AWS log group(s) an application is writing to.
Note: Multiple log groups must be supported for cases like multi-container applications, where a single application has sidecar containers, and each write to their own log group.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_LOG_GROUP_ARNS
documentation:
The Amazon Resource Name(s) (ARN) of the AWS log group(s).
Note: See the [log group ARN format documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/iam-access-control-overview-cwl.html#CWL_ARN_Format).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_LOG_STREAM_NAMES
documentation: The name(s) of the AWS log stream(s) an application is writing to.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.AWS_LOG_STREAM_ARNS
documentation:
The ARN(s) of the AWS log stream(s).
Note: See the [log stream ARN format documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/iam-access-control-overview-cwl.html#CWL_ARN_Format). One log group can contain several log streams, so these ARNs necessarily identify both a log group and a log stream.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.GCP_CLOUD_RUN_JOB_EXECUTION
documentation: The name of the Cloud Run [execution](https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/managing/job-executions) being run for the Job, as set by the [`CLOUD_RUN_EXECUTION`](https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/container-contract#jobs-env-vars) environment variable.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.GCP_CLOUD_RUN_JOB_TASK_INDEX
documentation: The index for a task within an execution as provided by the [`CLOUD_RUN_TASK_INDEX`](https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/container-contract#jobs-env-vars) environment variable.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.GCP_GCE_INSTANCE_NAME
documentation: The instance name of a GCE instance. This is the value provided by `host.name`, the visible name of the instance in the Cloud Console UI, and the prefix for the default hostname of the instance as defined by the [default internal DNS name](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/internal-dns#instance-fully-qualified-domain-names).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.GCP_GCE_INSTANCE_HOSTNAME
documentation: The hostname of a GCE instance. This is the full value of the default or [custom hostname](https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/instances/custom-hostname-vm).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.HEROKU_RELEASE_CREATION_TIMESTAMP
documentation: Time and date the release was created.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.HEROKU_RELEASE_COMMIT
documentation: Commit hash for the current release.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.HEROKU_APP_ID
documentation: Unique identifier for the application.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CONTAINER_NAME
documentation: Container name used by container runtime.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CONTAINER_ID
documentation: Container ID. Usually a UUID, as for example used to [identify Docker containers](https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/run/#container-identification). The UUID might be abbreviated.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CONTAINER_RUNTIME
documentation: The container runtime managing this container.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CONTAINER_IMAGE_NAME
documentation: Name of the image the container was built on.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CONTAINER_IMAGE_TAG
documentation: Container image tag.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CONTAINER_IMAGE_ID
documentation:
Runtime specific image identifier. Usually a hash algorithm followed by a UUID.
Note: Docker defines a sha256 of the image id; `container.image.id` corresponds to the `Image` field from the Docker container inspect [API](https://docs.docker.com/engine/api/v1.43/#tag/Container/operation/ContainerInspect) endpoint.
K8s defines a link to the container registry repository with digest `"imageID": "registry.azurecr.io /namespace/service/dockerfile@sha256:bdeabd40c3a8a492eaf9e8e44d0ebbb84bac7ee25ac0cf8a7159d25f62555625"`.
OCI defines a digest of manifest.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CONTAINER_COMMAND
documentation:
The command used to run the container (i.e. the command name).
Note: If using embedded credentials or sensitive data, it is recommended to remove them to prevent potential leakage.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CONTAINER_COMMAND_LINE
documentation: The full command run by the container as a single string representing the full command. [2].

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.CONTAINER_COMMAND_ARGS
documentation: All the command arguments (including the command/executable itself) run by the container. [2].

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.DEPLOYMENT_ENVIRONMENT
documentation: Name of the [deployment environment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deployment_environment) (aka deployment tier).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.DEVICE_ID
documentation:
A unique identifier representing the device.
Note: The device identifier MUST only be defined using the values outlined below. This value is not an advertising identifier and MUST NOT be used as such. On iOS (Swift or Objective-C), this value MUST be equal to the [vendor identifier](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uidevice/1620059-identifierforvendor). On Android (Java or Kotlin), this value MUST be equal to the Firebase Installation ID or a globally unique UUID which is persisted across sessions in your application. More information can be found [here](https://developer.android.com/training/articles/user-data-ids) on best practices and exact implementation details. Caution should be taken when storing personal data or anything which can identify a user. GDPR and data protection laws may apply, ensure you do your own due diligence.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.DEVICE_MODEL_IDENTIFIER
documentation:
The model identifier for the device.
Note: It's recommended this value represents a machine readable version of the model identifier rather than the market or consumer-friendly name of the device.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.DEVICE_MODEL_NAME
documentation:
The marketing name for the device model.
Note: It's recommended this value represents a human readable version of the device model rather than a machine readable alternative.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.DEVICE_MANUFACTURER
documentation:
The name of the device manufacturer.
Note: The Android OS provides this field via [Build](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/os/Build#MANUFACTURER). iOS apps SHOULD hardcode the value `Apple`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.FAAS_NAME
documentation:
The name of the single function that this runtime instance executes.
Note: This is the name of the function as configured/deployed on the FaaS
platform and is usually different from the name of the callback
function (which may be stored in the
[`code.namespace`/`code.function`](/docs/general/general-attributes.md#source-code-attributes)
span attributes).

For some cloud providers, the above definition is ambiguous. The following
definition of function name MUST be used for this attribute
(and consequently the span name) for the listed cloud providers/products:

* **Azure:**  The full name `<FUNCAPP>/<FUNC>`, i.e., function app name
  followed by a forward slash followed by the function name (this form
  can also be seen in the resource JSON for the function).
  This means that a span attribute MUST be used, as an Azure function
  app can host multiple functions that would usually share
  a TracerProvider (see also the `cloud.resource_id` attribute).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.FAAS_VERSION
documentation:
The immutable version of the function being executed.
Note: Depending on the cloud provider and platform, use:

* **AWS Lambda:** The [function version](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/lambda/latest/dg/configuration-versions.html)
  (an integer represented as a decimal string).
* **Google Cloud Run (Services):** The [revision](https://cloud.google.com/run/docs/managing/revisions)
  (i.e., the function name plus the revision suffix).
* **Google Cloud Functions:** The value of the
  [`K_REVISION` environment variable](https://cloud.google.com/functions/docs/env-var#runtime_environment_variables_set_automatically).
* **Azure Functions:** Not applicable. Do not set this attribute.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.FAAS_INSTANCE
documentation:
The execution environment ID as a string, that will be potentially reused for other invocations to the same function/function version.
Note: * **AWS Lambda:** Use the (full) log stream name.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.FAAS_MAX_MEMORY
documentation:
The amount of memory available to the serverless function converted to Bytes.
Note: It's recommended to set this attribute since e.g. too little memory can easily stop a Java AWS Lambda function from working correctly. On AWS Lambda, the environment variable `AWS_LAMBDA_FUNCTION_MEMORY_SIZE` provides this information (which must be multiplied by 1,048,576).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.HOST_ID
documentation: Unique host ID. For Cloud, this must be the instance_id assigned by the cloud provider. For non-containerized systems, this should be the `machine-id`. See the table below for the sources to use to determine the `machine-id` based on operating system.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.HOST_NAME
documentation: Name of the host. On Unix systems, it may contain what the hostname command returns, or the fully qualified hostname, or another name specified by the user.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.HOST_TYPE
documentation: Type of host. For Cloud, this must be the machine type.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.HOST_ARCH
documentation: The CPU architecture the host system is running on.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.HOST_IMAGE_NAME
documentation: Name of the VM image or OS install the host was instantiated from.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.HOST_IMAGE_ID
documentation: VM image ID or host OS image ID. For Cloud, this value is from the provider.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.HOST_IMAGE_VERSION
documentation: The version string of the VM image or host OS as defined in [Version Attributes](README.md#version-attributes).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_CLUSTER_NAME
documentation: The name of the cluster.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_CLUSTER_UID
documentation:
A pseudo-ID for the cluster, set to the UID of the `kube-system` namespace.
Note: K8s does not have support for obtaining a cluster ID. If this is ever
added, we will recommend collecting the `k8s.cluster.uid` through the
official APIs. In the meantime, we are able to use the `uid` of the
`kube-system` namespace as a proxy for cluster ID. Read on for the
rationale.

Every object created in a K8s cluster is assigned a distinct UID. The
`kube-system` namespace is used by Kubernetes itself and will exist
for the lifetime of the cluster. Using the `uid` of the `kube-system`
namespace is a reasonable proxy for the K8s ClusterID as it will only
change if the cluster is rebuilt. Furthermore, Kubernetes UIDs are
UUIDs as standardized by
[ISO/IEC 9834-8 and ITU-T X.667](https://www.itu.int/ITU-T/studygroups/com17/oid.html).
Which states:

> If generated according to one of the mechanisms defined in Rec.
  ITU-T X.667 | ISO/IEC 9834-8, a UUID is either guaranteed to be
  different from all other UUIDs generated before 3603 A.D., or is
  extremely likely to be different (depending on the mechanism chosen).

Therefore, UIDs between clusters should be extremely unlikely to
conflict.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_NODE_NAME
documentation: The name of the Node.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_NODE_UID
documentation: The UID of the Node.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_NAMESPACE_NAME
documentation: The name of the namespace that the pod is running in.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_POD_UID
documentation: The UID of the Pod.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_POD_NAME
documentation: The name of the Pod.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_CONTAINER_NAME
documentation: The name of the Container from Pod specification, must be unique within a Pod. Container runtime usually uses different globally unique name (`container.name`).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_CONTAINER_RESTART_COUNT
documentation: Number of times the container was restarted. This attribute can be used to identify a particular container (running or stopped) within a container spec.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_REPLICASET_UID
documentation: The UID of the ReplicaSet.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_REPLICASET_NAME
documentation: The name of the ReplicaSet.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_DEPLOYMENT_UID
documentation: The UID of the Deployment.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_DEPLOYMENT_NAME
documentation: The name of the Deployment.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_STATEFULSET_UID
documentation: The UID of the StatefulSet.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_STATEFULSET_NAME
documentation: The name of the StatefulSet.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_DAEMONSET_UID
documentation: The UID of the DaemonSet.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_DAEMONSET_NAME
documentation: The name of the DaemonSet.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_JOB_UID
documentation: The UID of the Job.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_JOB_NAME
documentation: The name of the Job.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_CRONJOB_UID
documentation: The UID of the CronJob.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.K8S_CRONJOB_NAME
documentation: The name of the CronJob.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.OS_TYPE
documentation: The operating system type.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.OS_DESCRIPTION
documentation: Human readable (not intended to be parsed) OS version information, like e.g. reported by `ver` or `lsb_release -a` commands.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.OS_NAME
documentation: Human readable operating system name.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.OS_VERSION
documentation: The version string of the operating system as defined in [Version Attributes](/docs/resource/README.md#version-attributes).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_PID
documentation: Process identifier (PID).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_PARENT_PID
documentation: Parent Process identifier (PID).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_EXECUTABLE_NAME
documentation: The name of the process executable. On Linux based systems, can be set to the `Name` in `proc/[pid]/status`. On Windows, can be set to the base name of `GetProcessImageFileNameW`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_EXECUTABLE_PATH
documentation: The full path to the process executable. On Linux based systems, can be set to the target of `proc/[pid]/exe`. On Windows, can be set to the result of `GetProcessImageFileNameW`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_COMMAND
documentation: The command used to launch the process (i.e. the command name). On Linux based systems, can be set to the zeroth string in `proc/[pid]/cmdline`. On Windows, can be set to the first parameter extracted from `GetCommandLineW`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_COMMAND_LINE
documentation: The full command used to launch the process as a single string representing the full command. On Windows, can be set to the result of `GetCommandLineW`. Do not set this if you have to assemble it just for monitoring; use `process.command_args` instead.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_COMMAND_ARGS
documentation: All the command arguments (including the command/executable itself) as received by the process. On Linux-based systems (and some other Unixoid systems supporting procfs), can be set according to the list of null-delimited strings extracted from `proc/[pid]/cmdline`. For libc-based executables, this would be the full argv vector passed to `main`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_OWNER
documentation: The username of the user that owns the process.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_RUNTIME_NAME
documentation: The name of the runtime of this process. For compiled native binaries, this SHOULD be the name of the compiler.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_RUNTIME_VERSION
documentation: The version of the runtime of this process, as returned by the runtime without modification.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.PROCESS_RUNTIME_DESCRIPTION
documentation: An additional description about the runtime of the process, for example a specific vendor customization of the runtime environment.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.SERVICE_NAME
documentation:
Logical name of the service.
Note: MUST be the same for all instances of horizontally scaled services. If the value was not specified, SDKs MUST fallback to `unknown_service:` concatenated with [`process.executable.name`](process.md#process), e.g. `unknown_service:bash`. If `process.executable.name` is not available, the value MUST be set to `unknown_service`.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.SERVICE_VERSION
documentation: The version string of the service API or implementation. The format is not defined by these conventions.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.SERVICE_NAMESPACE
documentation:
A namespace for `service.name`.
Note: A string value having a meaning that helps to distinguish a group of services, for example the team name that owns a group of services. `service.name` is expected to be unique within the same namespace. If `service.namespace` is not specified in the Resource then `service.name` is expected to be unique for all services that have no explicit namespace defined (so the empty/unspecified namespace is simply one more valid namespace). Zero-length namespace string is assumed equal to unspecified namespace.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.SERVICE_INSTANCE_ID
documentation:
The string ID of the service instance.
Note: MUST be unique for each instance of the same `service.namespace,service.name` pair (in other words `service.namespace,service.name,service.instance.id` triplet MUST be globally unique). The ID helps to distinguish instances of the same service that exist at the same time (e.g. instances of a horizontally scaled service). It is preferable for the ID to be persistent and stay the same for the lifetime of the service instance, however it is acceptable that the ID is ephemeral and changes during important lifetime events for the service (e.g. service restarts). If the service has no inherent unique ID that can be used as the value of this attribute it is recommended to generate a random Version 1 or Version 4 RFC 4122 UUID (services aiming for reproducible UUIDs may also use Version 5, see RFC 4122 for more recommendations).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.TELEMETRY_SDK_NAME
documentation:
The name of the telemetry SDK as defined above.
Note: The OpenTelemetry SDK MUST set the `telemetry.sdk.name` attribute to `opentelemetry`.
If another SDK, like a fork or a vendor-provided implementation, is used, this SDK MUST set the
`telemetry.sdk.name` attribute to the fully-qualified class or module name of this SDK's main entry point
or another suitable identifier depending on the language.
The identifier `opentelemetry` is reserved and MUST NOT be used in this case.
All custom identifiers SHOULD be stable across different versions of an implementation.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.TELEMETRY_SDK_LANGUAGE
documentation: The language of the telemetry SDK.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.TELEMETRY_SDK_VERSION
documentation: The version string of the telemetry SDK.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.TELEMETRY_AUTO_VERSION
documentation: The version string of the auto instrumentation agent, if used.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.WEBENGINE_NAME
documentation: The name of the web engine.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.WEBENGINE_VERSION
documentation: The version of the web engine.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.WEBENGINE_DESCRIPTION
documentation: Additional description of the web engine (e.g. detailed version and edition information).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.OTEL_SCOPE_NAME
documentation: The name of the instrumentation scope - (`InstrumentationScope.Name` in OTLP).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.OTEL_SCOPE_VERSION
documentation: The version of the instrumentation scope - (`InstrumentationScope.Version` in OTLP).

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.OTEL_LIBRARY_NAME
documentation: Deprecated, use the `otel.scope.name` attribute.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.OTEL_LIBRARY_VERSION
documentation: Deprecated, use the `otel.scope.version` attribute.

class_variables.name: opentelemetry.semconv.resource.ResourceAttributes.FAAS_ID
documentation: Deprecated, use the `cloud.resource.id` attribute.
